Vancouver Sun Run: Sign up & event info

Premiers amp up the fight with Ottawa over health-care spending   The country's 13 premiers are in Victoria for a second day as they discuss health care, inflation and post-pandemic economic recovery.
Bamboo Battle: B.C. homeowner wins $2,000 from neighbour who planted bamboo that invaded his lawn
Formula E: After pledge of July refunds, no word yet from organizers of nixed Vancouver race
Postmedia appeals judge's refusal to release information about secret B.C. trial
NASA's hot dust, steamy photos with Canadian connection capture the world's attention

Pulling Together: An eight-day Shuswap canoe journey drawing in Indigenous, law enforcement and public service communities   "For me, it's heartwarming to see like-minded individuals come together for the betterment of Indigenous communities and their relationships with public services." — Kyle Crump, a youth recreation manager with the Splatsin in Enderby
Major crime unit investigating disappearance of Port Alberni woman   Amber Manthorne was last seen on July 7 and her white Jeep was found south of Nanaimo a couple of days later
Royal no more: New Westminster to drop the 'Royal City' as its brand   The nickname/brand dates back 163 years, to when New Westminster was only a year old

Demand for retail space is bouncing back in some Vancouver neighbourhoods: report   Louwella Malda and Kyle Roberts launched The Filipino Noodle Joint in a mall on Keefer Street in Chinatown that has one of the highest vacancies in an especially hard hit area.

B.C. Real Estate: Rising interest rates lead to slower sales as home prices drop   The slowdown in sales appears to be having an effect on prices, which have started trending downward, according to BCREA data.
Bamboo Battle: B.C. homeowner wins $2,000 from neighbour who planted bamboo that invaded his lawn
COVID-19 update for July 12: WHO says COVID-19 remains a global health emergency | Alberta changing how it tests people for virus | Yet another Omicron variant has emerged
Premiers amp up the fight with Ottawa over health-care spending
B.C. Real Estate: Rising interest rates lead to slower sales as home prices drop
Traffic alert: Highway 1 westbound lanes blocked after accident damages 192nd Street overpass

CRTC demands details behind Rogers outage, Parliamentary committee to meet   The statement said the CRTC would 'determine what additional measures need to be taken' once it receives a satisfactory response from Rogers

'Frank's chapter is closed': Family, first responders reflect on search for missing boy   Frank, who was due to start Kindergarten in the fall, was a creative little boy who loved hugs, dancing and playing with his siblings.
Ombudsman finds CBC show didn't violate journalistic standards   The report comes after one questioner — Winnpeg data analyst Taylor Lakhryst — complained that show producers rewrote her questions.
Manitoba RCMP nab two B.C. men after drone delivers drugs to prison   A 20-year-old from Vancouver and a 22-year-old from Burnaby are facing drug trafficking and firearm charges.
Twitter sues Elon Musk to hold him to $44 billion merger   On Friday, Musk said he was terminating the deal.
'Behind the curve' Bank of Canada tees up super-sized 75 basis point hike   Expected to bring rate to 2.25 per cent — the largest increase since 1998
Half of Canadian and U.S. small businesses expect a recession this year, poll finds   About three in five respondents say they don’t see a resolution to inflation this year

Rogers CEO admits outage caused by maintenance operation   The cause: “a network system failure following a maintenance update in our core network, which caused some of our routers to malfunction early Friday morning.”
Posthaste: Why this mega-bull still believes oil is in the strongest fundamental market set up 'in decades, maybe ever'   'Resource scarcity is real this time around'
'A brutal summer': Air Canada paying the price for airport chaos   Shares have fallen 23% in three months for second biggest decline in the Bloomberg Americas Airlines Index
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.
Stephen Schneider: Report doubles down on enforcement measures that have failed to curb money laundering   Opinion: Curbing money laundering requires a new approach not wedded to past failures

John Ivison: Refreshing realism on the need for Canadian oil — from a Trudeau Liberal   Opinion: 'At the end of the day, the cause of climate change is not fossil fuels themselves. It is the carbon emissions associated with the burning of fossil fuels,' says minister of natural resources, Jonathan Wilkinson
Vaughn Palmer: Expect government secrecy to continue if David Eby becomes B.C. premier   Opinion: B.C's housing minister has become adept at after-hours press releases

Letters to The Sun, July 12, 2022: Vancouver Plan doesn't have all the answers   With hundreds, if not thousands of rental apartment units in towers already approved or in the development pipeline, and waiting to be built, we need to ask “Are these really just being built for investors?, asks reader Jan Pierce

Ian Mulgrew: ICBC dumpster-fire fix ruled unconstitutional — again   "The thinly veiled purpose of this legislation is to improve the finances of ICBC by reducing the quantity of expert evidence in motor vehicle accident liability claims and to thus both reduce litigation costs and produce lower damage awards whether by way of settlement or at trial." — Justice Nathan Smith

Canucks: Free-agency swing will net a single or a double, but no home runs   'We like J.T. There’s no panic to move him until probably a month before the trading deadline — if he wasn’t re-signed.' — Canucks president of hockey operations Jim Rutherford.

Canucks: Linus Karlsson aims to please after record-setting SHL rookie season   In a touch of trade irony, Linus Karlsson takes the next pro step with Canucks, while Jonathan Dahlen wasn't qualified by the Sharks.

Ty Ronning considering all his options, but it would be 'pretty cool' to sign with Canucks   Vancouver Canucks had a chance to get Ty Ronning twice in the seventh round of the 2016 NHL Draft but passed on him and the Vancouver Giants star was picked by the New York Rangers
Walking wounded Whitecaps saunter into Cincinnati    The Vancouver Whitecaps will be without nine regulars on Wednesday when they travel to Cincinnati

Big hitter going, going gone for Vancouver Canadians, slugger Addison Barger gets promotion   Teenage left-hander Ricky Tiedemann, who's pitched well enough since coming to Vancouver that he's in the Futures Game in L.A on Saturday, could easily be next in line to move up
Britt Daniel spends some time with Lucifer on the Sofa   Minimalist rockers Spoon are back touring and leader Britt Daniel loves it.
Vancouver Queer Film Festival announces line-up for 34th event   Dave Rodden-Shortt’s feature documentary The Empress of Vancouver is the star of the VQFF's opening gala
Disney on Ice returns to PNE in Vancouver for first time since 2019   Disney On Ice is back with a new show, Road Trip Adventures, at the PNE's Pacific Coliseum in November
After two-year hiatus, the Vancouver Folk Music Festival twangs back with star-studded lineup   The 45th annual folk festival has an incredible lineup of artists.
Succession, Ted Lasso lead nominations for Emmy awards   Succession received a leading 25 Emmy nominations, while Ted Lasso was the top comedy series nominee with 20 bids.

Style File: Four cool travel finds for your summer adventures   If your summer season is shaping up to be action-packed, there are a number of new releases hitting stores designed to make your upcoming adventures even better.
Cook this: Apricot BBQ sticky ribs from Peak Season   ‘An apricot will bring out the best of these grilled rib’s smoky flavours,’ says recipe developer, food writer and nutritionist Deirdre Buryk
Canada ranks among nations where people are most likely to live beyond 100: report   By 2065, there is projected to be over 87,500 centenarians in Canada
Beauty Bar: Dyson Airwrap Multi-styler Complete   A redesigned iteration of the original Dyson Airwrap hairstyling device that sees "re-engineered attachments powered by enhanced Coanda airflow."
Fitness: Dive in, but know how to use the water to your advantage   The pool is the coolest place to exercise during a hot summer and you don’t need to be a swimmer to reap the benefits.

Community group asks City of Vancouver to oppose rezoning after B.C. Housing review and board firings   The Kitsilano Coalition said Vancouver should ask B.C. Housing and its new board to review and make changes to the Arbutus social housing project.
Sold (Bought): Entertaining is front and centre in this West End condo   There's even a a martini bar in the kitchen

A Light Touch: A dated Savary Island vacation property becomes a dreamy-bright family retreat   West Coast boutique design firm Parker and Harlow recently designed this 2,500-square-feet waterfront vacation property off the coast of Powell River

Affidavits for Shangri-La tower class-action lawsuit examine market values   The case began in December 2015 when the lead plaintiff filed a notice of civil claim, alleging that defective glass windows were fogging, leaking and spontaneously breaking.
The Home Front: Motivated to do good work   Vancouver architects Tracey Mactavish and Asher deGroot, of Motiv Architects, aim to build community connections
Plenty of pubs and castles to enjoy in Ireland   Whether your tastes are modern or historic, high brow or low brow, the Emerald Isle has much to offer.
36 Hours in Istanbul    If Turkiye is the crown bridging east and west, Istanbul is its crown jewel
Full disclosure from a first-time cruiser   My first cruise ship experience left me …wondering why I've been holding back all these years."

Daphne Bramham: The old West makes way for a new, wilder one   Saskatchewan's Grasslands National Park offers a glimpse of the past (including wild bison roaming), but also a vision for a wilder future

Cancelled Air Canada flights strand Quebec teens at Vancouver airport   A teen scout group heading to the Yukon for a long-planned camping trip rolling ended up rolling out their sleeping bags on the floor of YVR after their Air Canada flight was cancelled
Quebec company takes Tesla Model S Plaid to 347 km/h   Software hacks permitted the car to blow past its 175-mph (281-km/h) limiter and crack the vaunted 200-mph barrier
Watch: Pilot lands disabled plane on North Carolina highway   Sheriff says it's "amazing" the man managed to dodge vehicles and power lines
Ka-Chow: Ford trademarks 'Lightning' for Ranger, Maverick in Europe   Could the filings suggest EV variants of these models? What else could a Ranger Lightning and Maverick Lightning be?
Walmart to electrify its delivery fleet with Canoo EVs   The deal gives Walmart an option to purchase up to 10,000 units
Family Vehicle Roundup: 2023 Honda HR-V, best rides for camping, and more   Because where are else are you going to find a list of the most famous RVs from the big screen?

Drinking alone when you're young linked to alcohol problems later in life   Researchers say young people often drink alone as a way to cope with negative emotions, a pattern of use that has been linked to alcohol use disorder.

Arthritis is so much more than just a few aches and pains   Since being diagnosed with idiopathic arthritis, Rachel Gehue has had fusions on both of her wrists, and her foot, as well as splints made for the fingers on both hands.

'Nature's life support system': Fake or real, houseplants are great for mental health   Since COVID began, Canada's indoor plant industry has been booming, with sales of potted plants jumping from more than $903 million in 2020 to more than $995 million in 2021.
Does mindfulness meditation really reduce pain?   Participants taught to meditate before experiencing painful stimuli reported a 33% drop in pain unpleasantness and 32% reduction in intensity.
What are the signs of Magnesium deficiency?   More than one-third of Canadian adults aren't getting enough magnesium.
Canadian company launches pocket-sized electronic dispenser for liquid concentrates   The company-described “Keurig of cannabis” measures cannabis concentrates in 1 mg intervals.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
Ozzy Osbourne drops $1k at cannabis dispensary and then decides not to smoke   The Black Sabbath frontman has been sober for almost a decade.
Ontario police seize cannabis worth $61 million   Estimated 45,000-plus illegal pot plants and eight metric tonnes of processed weed confiscated.
Brittney Griner in the spotlight during WNBA All-Star game   “We are not going to stop until everyone understands how serious this really is.”
